commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Sécu reimburses 65% for diabetes indication; mutuelle covers rest. Without ALD, copay ~€10/mo.

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Pharmacies on nearly every block in French cities. Green cross signage. Pharmacists can dispense many medications with French prescription. EU prescriptions accepted.
medicine.notes: France Sécurité Sociale reimburses 65-100% of approved medications. With ALD (Affection Longue Durée) status for chronic conditions, reimbursement reaches 100%. Mutuelle (supplementary insurance) covers the rest. Prices shown are after typical Sécu + mutuelle reimbursement.

plans.name: Google Fi Flexible (US Number)
plans.notes: Line 1 $20 + Line 2 $15 = $35 base. Data $10/GB × 3GB = $30. Works in France at same rate. Keeps US number for banking/2FA.

plans.name: Free Mobile
plans.notes: 2 lines €9.99/line (~$11). Free Mobile best value. EU roaming included.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Menu du jour typically includes 2-3 courses. No tipping expected (service compris).
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant: menu du jour ~€28 (service included), 2x/week = ~€243/mo = ~$275/mo in Paris, France.

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: No specific senior discounts. Highly competitive market keeps prices low.
categories.notes: Free Freebox 1 Gig €24.99/mo or Orange Livebox Fibre €29.99/mo. Includes TV channels. ~$28-32/mo.

categories.name: NFL RedZone
categories.notes: DAZN NFL Game Pass €19.99/mo during season (Sep-Feb). Annualized ~€13/mo (~$15). Includes RedZone, live games.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: Municipal facilities offer senior rates. Basic Fit from €19.99/mo.
categories.notes: Basic Fit €19.99-29.99/mo, municipal pools and gyms. ~$22-34/mo.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: Most parks and gardens free. Senior transit passes for access.
categories.notes: Free parks, jardins publics, coastal walks, hiking trails.

categories.name: Books/Media
categories.seniorDiscounts: Médiathèques free with carte de résident.
categories.notes: Libraries (médiathèques) free. Budget for newspapers, books.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: No specific discounts. Café culture is affordable.

monthlyPass.notes: Paris transit pass. Seniors 65+ eligible for 30-50% reduced fare cards (carte senior). Must register at transit office.

publicTransit.coverage: Well-connected bus and tram network. TGV and regional rail for intercity travel.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ eligible for 30-50% reduced fare cards (carte senior). Must register at transit office.

rideShare.availability: Uber available in cities. Traditional taxis at stands. BlaBlaCar popular for intercity.

registration.notes: Carte grise (registration) is one-time. No annual vehicle tax. Contrôle technique (inspection) every 2 years.

parking.notes: Resident parking (stationnement résidentiel) significantly cheaper with proof of address.

tolls.notes: Estimated monthly toll/highway costs for Paris area.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Seniors 65+ eligible for 30-50% reduced fare cards (carte senior). Must register at transit office.

transportation.walkability: Good walkability in city centers. Most daily errands manageable on foot.

carFree.notes: Transit pass + occasional ride-share. Good for urban core living.

withCar.notes: Includes insurance, fuel, parking, maintenance. Higher urban parking and insurance costs.

eyeExam.notes: Exam ~25 EUR. Securite Sociale reimburses 70%, mutuelle covers remainder. Ophthalmologist visit may cost more if secteur 2.

glasses.notes: Glasses with mutuelle ~50-100 EUR after refund. 100% Sante reform guarantees a fully covered frame+lens option. Progressive lenses ~150 EUR.

insurance.coverage: Mutuelle complementaire covers vision copays + frame/lens allowance
insurance.notes: Mutuelle typically included as part of broader health complementaire (~40-60 EUR/mo total). Vision portion ~8 EUR/mo. 100% Sante ensures zero out-of-pocket for basic frames.

cleaning.notes: Detartrage (scaling) ~30 EUR. Securite Sociale covers 70% of tarif conventionnel. Mutuelle covers ticket moderateur.

filling.notes: Composite filling ~60 EUR. Well-covered under Securite Sociale + mutuelle. Depassements possible for some dentists.

crown.notes: Couronne ceramique ~500 EUR. RAC zero (100% Sante) available for certain crown types since 2020 reform. Otherwise mutuelle covers 200-300% base tariff.

insurance.coverage: Mutuelle complementaire dental, 2 cleanings/year, 100% Sante crowns/bridges
insurance.notes: Dental portion of mutuelle. 100% Sante reform guarantees zero-cost crowns, bridges, and dentures for regulated tariff options.

items.name: Water
items.notes: Tap safe in most areas; mineral preferred

items.name: Premium dog food
items.notes: Royal Canin/Hills from pet shops or vet clinics

breakdown.notes: Renter's insurance not standard in most European countries. Utilities billed separately.

housing.taxNotes: No rental income tax for tenants. Taxe d'habitation abolished for primary residences. Property tax (taxe foncière) is landlord responsibility.
housing.leaseTerms: 3-year unfurnished lease (bail de 3 ans, renewable). 1-year furnished lease. Security deposit 1 month rent (unfurnished) or 2 months (furnished). 3-month notice period for tenants.
housing.marketNotes: Most expensive French market. Apartments only at 2BR budget. Extremely competitive. Consider inner suburbs.

stores.name: Carrefour
stores.notes: Largest French chain. Hypermarkets, supermarkets, and Express formats.

stores.name: E.Leclerc
stores.notes: Cooperative chain, competitive prices. Drive-through pickup available.

stores.name: Lidl
stores.notes: Best prices on staples. Weekly themed promotions.

stores.name: Intermarché
stores.notes: Strong in rural areas, good quality own-brand products.

stores.name: Marchés locaux
stores.notes: Weekly outdoor markets in every town. Best for fresh produce, cheese, and local specialties.

healthcare.coverageSummary: France provides universal healthcare via Protection Universelle Maladie (PUMA). Sécu reimburses 70% of doctor visits, 65% of meds. Mutuelle (supplemental) covers the rest. As legal residents, retirees enroll after 3 months.

costs.notes: Comprehensive mutuelle for AMD coverage. Age-rated, ~€110/mo
Ophthalmologist for AMD monitoring. Secteur 1 rates.

blackHairCare.label: Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Limited Afro-textured hair salons outside Paris. Check Afro-coiffure salons in city centers.

providers.name: Afro-coiffure salons
providers.notes: Specialized in textured hair, locs, braids

providers.name: Local coiffeur
providers.notes: Standard French salon, may lack textured hair experience

stores.name: Zara / H&M / Uniqlo
stores.notes: Mid-range, available in all major cities

stores.name: Soldes (seasonal sales)
stores.notes: Government-regulated sales in Jan and June/July — 30-70% off

discounts.name: Carte Avantage Senior SNCF
discounts.description: Annual card (€49) for 30% off all TGV and Intercités trains. Available at 60+.

discounts.name: Carte Senior municipal transport
discounts.description: Reduced-rate or free local bus/tram pass for 65+ in most cities.

discounts.name: Museum/cinema discounts
discounts.description: Reduced entry (tarif réduit) at national museums, many cinemas, and cultural sites for 60+.

discounts.name: Allocation de Solidarité aux Personnes Âgées (ASPA)
discounts.description: Minimum pension guarantee for residents 65+ with low income (~€961/mo single, €1,492/mo couple).

discounts.name: Taxe foncière exemption
discounts.description: Property tax exemption for low-income residents 75+ on primary residence. Renters not directly affected but landlord savings may help.
